response.summary: P4 is the correct next phase after the shell and routing work in P3. It is well-bounded around the first real user-facing PARA CRUD flows, aligns with the locked stack and ADRs, and uses foundations already put in place in P2-P3. The main execution risks are around keeping form schemas and list/detail behaviors consistent across four entity types, but those risks are known and manageable without replanning.
key_risks: List/detail/create-edit/archive is being delivered for four entities at once, which raises the chance of uneven behavior or one entity lagging behind the others.
Using shared Zod schemas across API and forms can still drift in practice if create/update form needs are not clearly separated from response schemas.
URL-param sort/filter can become inconsistent across Areas, Projects, Tasks, and Resources unless parameter names and defaults are standardized up front.
Project hierarchy introduces extra UI and query complexity inside the same phase, especially if expandable tree behavior is treated as part of the MVP surface.
Inline validation parity between server errors and react-hook-form errors may be awkward unless error mapping is normalized early.
response.recommended_next_step: Proceed with P4, but define one shared contract first for query params, archive-toggle behavior, and form error mapping before implementing the four entity flows.
acceptance_checks: Create, list, detail, edit, and archive all work end-to-end for Areas against the real backend.
Create, list, detail, edit, and archive all work end-to-end for Projects against the real backend, including parent/child display if hierarchy is exposed.
Create, list, detail, edit, and archive all work end-to-end for Tasks against the real backend.
Create, list, detail, edit, and archive all work end-to-end for Resources against the real backend.
Archived entities are hidden by default and become visible only when the archived toggle is enabled.
Sort and filter state persists in the URL and survives refresh/navigation for every implemented list view.
Forms use shared Zod-backed validation and surface inline errors for invalid submissions.
At least one RTL smoke test covers a P4 list/form flow and passes.
open_questions: Are dedicated create/update form schemas already available in `packages/shared`, or will P4 need to introduce them alongside the pages?
What exact URL search-param names and default sort orders should be standardized across the four list views?
Is project hierarchy rendering in P4 limited to read-only expansion, or does this phase also include re-parenting from the UI?
Should archive actions be exposed as DELETE calls only, or is there also an explicit unarchive path expected in P4?
